question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the readmitTime.set(Instant.now().plus(streamProperties.getReadmitDelay())) assignment to create a predictable inactivity window, timing their attack to ensure the readmitDelay expires only after the staking period boundary has passed and the staking reward block is no longer the next expected block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node send a BLOCK_HEADER item followed by staking reward transaction items, then send a second BLOCK_HEADER before the first block is complete, triggering the BlockStreamException 'Received block items of a new block while the previous block is still pending' and causing the staking reward transfers in the first block to be discarded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node send block items without a BLOCK_HEADER as the first item (triggering 'Incorrect first block item case'), causing a BlockStreamException that increments the error counter and eventually marks the node inactive, preventing staking reward blocks from being processed during the readmitDelay window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the assembler.timeout() calculation (timeout.toMillis() - stopwatch.elapsed()) to send block items slowly enough that the remaining timeout approaches zero, then send the staking reward transaction items at the last moment, causing a timeout exception that drops the entire block including the staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that errors.set(0) only resets on successful block processing (not on partial success) to accumulate error counts across multiple blocks, reaching maxSubscribeAttempts precisely when a block containing staking reward transactions is being streamed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the DataSizeUnit(DataUnit.MEGABYTES) annotation to send a response that is exactly 36MB + 1 byte, causing the mirror node to reject the block containing staking reward transactions due to the maxStreamResponseSize limit being exceeded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the non-atomic nature of the active flag and readmitTime AtomicReference in BlockNode to create a race condition where tryReadmit() sets active=true but the node immediately fails on the staking reward block, causing the reward to be missed before the node is re-deactivated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that the pending list in BlockAssembler is never persisted to disk, meaning that if a BlockStreamException is thrown after partial block assembly (including staking reward items), those items are lost in memory and never retried, permanently omitting the staking rewards from the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that maxSubscribeAttempts defaults to 3 and the error counter is per-node (not per-block) to cause 3 failures on non-reward blocks, mark the node inactive, and then ensure the staking reward block is only available from that now-inactive node, causing the reward to be permanently missed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the 400ms responseTimeout to perform a timing attack where the serverStatus response for the staking reward block is delayed by exactly 401ms, causing the mirror node to treat the block as unavailable and fall back to a file source that may also be unavailable, resulting in the staking reward block being skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the integer overflow risk in pendingCount (int type) by sending Integer.MAX_VALUE - maxBlockItems + 1 block items across multiple BlockItemSets, causing pendingCount to overflow and bypass the maxBlockItems check, allowing an arbitrarily large block to be processed with potentially corrupted staking reward data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the DataSize comparison to send a block response where the staking reward transactions are embedded in a compressed payload that appears under maxStreamResponseSize but expands to a size that causes memory exhaustion during deserialization, preventing the staking rewards from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the readmitDelay minimum of 10 seconds (@DurationMin(seconds=10)) combined with maxSubscribeAttempts=1 to create a scenario where a single failed connection attempt during a staking reward block causes a 10-second inactivity window, during which the staking period boundary passes and the reward block is no longer the next expected block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that the BlockStreamException thrown when maxBlockItems is exceeded calls onError(), which increments the error counter, to chain multiple oversized block responses and exhaust maxSubscribeAttempts, marking the node inactive precisely when the staking reward block needs to be processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the grpcCall.read(assembler.timeout(), TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS) timeout in streamBlocks() to send block items for a staking reward block at a rate just slow enough to exhaust the remaining timeout, causing the read to return null and the block (with its staking rewards) to be abandoned mid-assembly?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that pending.clear() and pendingCount=0 only happen in onEndOfBlock(), meaning that if a BlockStreamException is thrown before END_OF_BLOCK is received (e.g., due to maxBlockItems), the staking reward items already in the pending list are silently discarded without any recovery mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that the errors counter is reset to 0 only on successful block processing (errors.set(0) inside the while loop) to maintain a persistent error count of maxSubscribeAttempts-1 across many blocks, then trigger a single failure on the staking reward block to immediately mark the node inactive?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the Instant.now().isAfter(readmitTime.get()) check in tryReadmit() to perform a time-based attack where the node is readmitted just before the staking reward block but immediately fails again, cycling through the readmitDelay window multiple times and ensuring the staking reward block is never successfully processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize is enforced at the gRPC transport layer (not at the application layer) to send a block containing staking reward transactions where the protobuf encoding of the staking reward items alone exceeds 36MB, causing the entire block to be rejected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that the BlockAssembler does not validate the block number continuity between consecutive blocks to send a block with a skipped block number that contains staking reward transactions, causing the mirror node to process an incorrect block sequence and potentially miss or double-count staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that responseTimeout applies to the serverStatus (hasBlock) check but not to the actual block streaming, to pass the availability check for a staking reward block but then stall the streaming indefinitely, causing the assembler timeout to expire and the staking rewards to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that maxSubscribeAttempts is checked against a per-node AtomicInteger (errors) that is not persisted across mirror node restarts, to cause the mirror node to restart (e.g., via OOM from a large block) and reset the error counter, allowing the attacker to repeatedly fail on staking reward blocks without ever triggering the inactivity mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that the BlockAssembler's pending list grows unboundedly until the maxBlockItems check is triggered, to send a block with staking reward transactions embedded at position maxBlockItems-1, causing the check to pass but the subsequent item to trigger rejection, leaving the staking rewards in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that readmitDelay is applied uniformly to all failure types (including BlockStreamException from maxBlockItems, responseTimeout, and maxStreamResponseSize) to create a compound attack where multiple different limit violations are used to keep all block nodes inactive during the staking reward payout window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a block node exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize defaults to 36MB but the @DataSizeUnit(DataUnit.MEGABYTES) annotation means the value is interpreted in megabytes, to send a response of exactly 36,700,160 bytes (35MB in binary) that passes the check but contains malformed staking reward transaction data that causes incorrect reward amounts to be recorded?
